steps.title: Identify what language shows understanding
steps.content: The claim is that Whitman “deeply understands the reader.” This requires a quotation where Whitman expresses direct perception or knowledge of something essential about the reader.

steps.title: Match the language to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A—“Your true soul and body appear before me”—shows Whitman perceiving the reader's essential self; “appear” signals his ability to see and know them.

Choices B, C, and D express assertion and transformation (B), regret (C), or doubt (D), not understanding.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
